notableMoments: Became the first African team to win a World Cup match when they beat Mexico 3-1 at the 1978 World Cup in Argentina, a landmark achievement for the continent
Beat France 1-0 at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar with a goal from Wahbi Khazri, though they were ultimately eliminated in the group stage
Known as the Eagles of Carthage, Tunisia have been one of the most consistent African qualifiers for the World Cup over the past three decades
The 1978 World Cup squad is revered in Tunisian football history as pioneers who put African football on the global map
Won the 2004 Africa Cup of Nations on home soil, the greatest achievement in Tunisian football and a source of immense national pride
Despite never advancing past the group stage, Tunisia have been competitive in every World Cup they have attended, often missing out by the smallest of margins
The Tunisian football diaspora in France has produced numerous talented players who have represented the national team, including Skhiri, Khazri, and Mejbri
Qualifying for the 2026 World Cup continues Tunisia's tradition as one of Africa's leading football nations and most reliable World Cup participants
